The result is a reproducible, operationally grounded framework for deciding whether an LLM or RAG system is ready to ship.

zh.title: LLM就绪性工具：用于LLM/RAG应用的评估、可观测性和CI门控
zh.summary: LLM就绪性工具集成了评估、可观测性和CI门控，以确定LLM和RAG系统在多个维度上是否生产就绪。在票证路由和接地任务上的测试表明该系统可以阻止不安全的部署。

fr.title: Harnais de Disponibilité LLM : Évaluation, Observabilité et Portes CI pour les Applications LLM/RAG
fr.summary: Le Harnais de Disponibilité LLM intègre l'évaluation, l'observabilité et les portes CI pour déterminer si les systèmes LLM et RAG sont prêts pour la production dans plusieurs dimensions. Les tests sur le routage des tickets et les tâches d'ancrage démontrent que le système peut bloquer les déploiements non sécurisés.

de.title: LLM-Bereitschafts-Tool: Bewertung, Observability und CI Gates für LLM/RAG-Anwendungen
de.summary: Das LLM-Bereitschafts-Tool integriert Bewertung, Observability und CI Gates, um festzustellen, ob LLM- und RAG-Systeme über mehrere Dimensionen hinweg produktionsreif sind. Tests zu Ticket-Routing und Verankerungsaufgaben zeigen, dass das System unsichere Bereitstellungen blockieren kann.

es.title: Arnés de Disponibilidad LLM: Evaluación, Observabilidad y Compuertas CI para Aplicaciones LLM/RAG
es.summary: El Arnés de Disponibilidad LLM integra evaluación, observabilidad y compuertas CI para determinar si los sistemas LLM y RAG están listos para producción en múltiples dimensiones. Las pruebas en tareas de enrutamiento de tickets y anclaje demuestran que el sistema puede bloquear implementaciones inseguras.

title: Defend: Automated Rebuttals for Peer Review with Minimal Author Guidance
summary: DEFEND is an LLM tool that helps authors generate factually accurate rebuttals to peer review comments through guided reasoning with minimal intervention. The system outperforms direct rebuttal generation and reduces author cognitive load.
content:
arXiv:2603.27360v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Rebuttal generation is a critical component of the peer review process for scientific papers, enabling authors to clarify misunderstandings, correct factual inaccuracies, and guide reviewers toward a more accurate evaluation. We observe that Large Language Models (LLMs) often struggle to perform targeted refutation and maintain accurate factual grounding when used directly for rebuttal generation, highlighting the need for structured reasoning and author intervention. To address this, in the paper, we introduce DEFEND an LLM based tool designed to explicitly execute the underlying reasoning process of automated rebuttal generation, while keeping the author-in-the-loop. As opposed to writing the rebuttals from scratch, the author needs to only drive the reasoning process with minimal intervention, leading an efficient approach with minimal effort and less cognitive load. We compare DEFEND against three other paradigms: (i) Direct rebuttal generation using LLM (DRG), (ii) Segment-wise rebuttal generation using LLM (SWRG), and (iii) Sequential approach (SA) of segment-wise rebuttal generation without author intervention. To enable finegrained evaluation, we extend the ReviewCritique dataset, creating review segmentation, deficiency, error type annotations, rebuttal-action labels, and mapping to gold rebuttal segments. Experimental results and a user study demonstrate that directly using LLMs perform poorly in factual correctness and targeted refutation. Segment-wise generation and the automated sequential approach with author-in-the-loop, substantially improve factual correctness and strength of refutation.

zh.title: Defend：通过最少作者指导为同行评审生成自动反驳
zh.summary: DEFEND是一个LLM工具，通过引导推理和最少干预，帮助作者针对同行评审意见生成事实准确的反驳。该系统优于直接反驳生成，并减少了作者的认知负担。

fr.title: Defend : Répliques Automatisées pour l'Examen par les Pairs avec Guidance Minimale de l'Auteur
fr.summary: DEFEND est un outil LLM qui aide les auteurs à générer des répliques factuellement précises aux commentaires d'examen par les pairs par le biais d'un raisonnement guidé avec une intervention minimale. Le système surpasse la génération directe de répliques et réduit la charge cognitive des auteurs.

de.title: Defend: Automatisierte Gegenargumente für die Peer Review mit minimaler Autorenanleitung
de.summary: DEFEND ist ein LLM-Tool, das Autoren hilft, durch gesteuertes Denken mit minimalem Eingriff sachlich genaue Gegenargumente zu Peer-Review-Kommentaren zu generieren. Das System übertrifft die direkte Generierung von Gegenargumenten und reduziert die kognitive Belastung der Autoren.

es.title: Defend: Contrarréplicas Automatizadas para Revisión por Pares con Mínima Orientación del Autor
es.summary: DEFEND es una herramienta LLM que ayuda a los autores a generar contrarréplicas factualmente precisas a los comentarios de revisión por pares mediante razonamiento guiado con intervención mínima. El sistema supera la generación directa de contrarréplicas y reduce la carga cognitiva del autor.

title: Heterogeneous Debate Engine: Identity-Grounded Cognitive Architecture for Resilient LLM-Based Ethical Tutoring
summary: The Heterogeneous Debate Engine combines identity-grounded retrieval-augmented generation with opponent modeling to enable LLM-based ethical tutoring that maintains doctrinal consistency. Testing shows this architecture significantly improves educational outcomes over baselines.
content:
arXiv:2603.27404v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are being increasingly used as autonomous agents in complex reasoning tasks, opening the niche for dialectical interactions. However, Multi-Agent systems implemented with systematically unconstrained systems systematically undergo semantic drift and logical deterioration and thus can hardly be used in providing ethical tutoring where a precise answer is required. Current simulation often tends to degenerate into dialectical stagnation, the agents degenerate into recursive concurrence or circular arguments. A critical challenge remains: how to enforce doctrinal fidelity without suppressing the generative flexibility required for dialectical reasoning? To address this niche, we contribute the Heterogeneous Debate Engine (HDE), a cognitive architecture that combines Identity-Grounded Retrieval-Augmented Generation (ID-RAG) for doctrinal fidelity and Heuristic Theory of Mind for strategic opponent modeling. Our evaluation shows that architectural heterogeneity is a crucial variable to stability: contrary doctrinal initializations (e.g., Deontology vs. Utilitarianism) have increased the Argument Complexity Scores of students by an order of magnitude, over baselines. These findings validate the effectiveness of ID-RAG and Heuristic ToM as architectural requirements in maintaining high-fidelity (adversarial) pedagogy.

zh.title: 异质辩论引擎：用于弹性LLM伦理辅导的身份基础认知架构
zh.summary: 异质辩论引擎将身份基础的检索增强生成与对手建模相结合，能够支持LLM伦理辅导，同时保持学说的一致性。测试表明该架构在教育成果上明显优于基准方法。

fr.title: Moteur de débat hétérogène : architecture cognitive fondée sur l'identité pour le tutorat éthique résilient basé sur LLM
fr.summary: Le moteur de débat hétérogène combine la génération augmentée par récupération fondée sur l'identité avec la modélisation des adversaires pour permettre le tutorat éthique basé sur LLM tout en maintenant la cohérence doctrinale. Les tests montrent que cette architecture améliore considérablement les résultats éducatifs par rapport aux référentiels.

de.title: Heterogenes Debattenmodul: Identitätsgestützte kognitive Architektur für widerstandsfähiges LLM-basiertes ethisches Tutoring
de.summary: Die heterogene Debattenmaschine kombiniert identitätsgestützte retrieval-augmented generation mit Opponent Modeling, um LLM-basiertes ethisches Tutoring zu ermöglichen, das die doktrinäre Konsistenz aufrechterhält. Tests zeigen, dass diese Architektur die Lernergebnisse deutlich gegenüber Baselines verbessert.

es.title: Motor de debate heterogéneo: arquitectura cognitiva basada en identidad para tutoría ética resiliente basada en LLM
es.summary: El motor de debate heterogéneo combina la generación aumentada por recuperación basada en identidad con el modelado de oponentes para permitir la tutoría ética basada en LLM manteniendo la consistencia doctrinal. Las pruebas muestran que esta arquitectura mejora significativamente los resultados educativos sobre las líneas de base.

title: On the Relationship between Bayesian Networks and Probabilistic Structural Causal Models
summary: This paper examines mathematical conditions for transforming Bayesian networks into probabilistic structural causal models and vice versa. Linear algebra and programming techniques establish existence and uniqueness of transformations while preserving model semantics.
content:
arXiv:2603.27406v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: In this paper, the relationship between probabilistic graphical models, in particular Bayesian networks, and causal diagrams, also called structural causal models, is studied. Structural causal models are deterministic models, based on structural equations or functions, that can be provided with uncertainty by adding independent, unobserved random variables to the models, equipped with probability distributions. One question that arises is whether a Bayesian network that has obtained from expert knowledge or learnt from data can be mapped to a probabilistic structural causal model, and whether or not this has consequences for the network structure and probability distribution. We show that linear algebra and linear programming offer key methods for the transformation, and examine properties for the existence and uniqueness of solutions based on dimensions of the probabilistic structural model. Finally, we examine in what way the semantics of the models is affected by this transformation.
  Keywords: Causality, probabilistic structural causal models, Bayesian networks, linear algebra, experimental software.

zh.title: 贝叶斯网络与概率结构因果模型的关系研究
zh.summary: 该论文研究了将贝叶斯网络转换为概率结构因果模型的数学条件，反之亦然。线性代数和编程技术建立了变换的存在性和唯一性，同时保留了模型语义。

fr.title: Sur la relation entre les réseaux bayésiens et les modèles causaux structurels probabilistes
fr.summary: Cet article examine les conditions mathématiques pour transformer les réseaux bayésiens en modèles causaux structurels probabilistes et vice versa. Les techniques d'algèbre linéaire et de programmation établissent l'existence et l'unicité des transformations tout en préservant la sémantique du modèle.

de.title: Über die Beziehung zwischen Bayesschen Netzwerken und probabilistischen strukturellen Kausalmodellen
de.summary: Das Papier untersucht mathematische Bedingungen für die Umwandlung von Bayesschen Netzwerken in probabilistische strukturelle Kausalmodelle und umgekehrt. Techniken der linearen Algebra und Programmierung etablieren Existenz und Eindeutigkeit von Transformationen, während die Modellsemantik erhalten bleibt.

es.title: Sobre la relación entre redes bayesianas y modelos causales estructurales probabilísticos
es.summary: Este artículo examina las condiciones matemáticas para transformar redes bayesianas en modelos causales estructurales probabilísticos y viceversa. Las técnicas de álgebra lineal y programación establecen la existencia y unicidad de transformaciones mientras preservan la semántica del modelo.

title: Greedy Is a Strong Default: Agents as Iterative Optimizers
summary: Replacing random proposals with LLM-guided reasoning in classical optimization algorithms, the paper shows greedy hill climbing matches or exceeds complex methods like simulated annealing. Results across tasks suggest LLM priors are strong enough that sophistication in acceptance rules provides limited benefit.
content:
arXiv:2603.27415v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Classical optimization algorithms--hill climbing, simulated annealing, population-based methods--generate candidate solutions via random perturbations. We replace the random proposal generator with an LLM agent that reasons about evaluation diagnostics to propose informed candidates, and ask: does the classical optimization machinery still help when the proposer is no longer random? We evaluate on four tasks spanning discrete, mixed, and continuous search spaces (all replicated across 3 independent runs): rule-based classification on Breast Cancer (test accuracy 86.0% to 96.5%), mixed hyperparameter optimization for MobileNetV3-Small on STL-10 (84.5% to 85.8%, zero catastrophic failures vs. 60% for random search), LoRA fine-tuning of Qwen2.5-0.5B on SST-2 (89.5% to 92.7%, matching Optuna TPE with 2x efficiency), and XGBoost on Adult Census (AUC 0.9297 to 0.9317, tying CMA-ES with 3x fewer evaluations). Empirically, on these tasks: a cross-task ablation shows that simulated annealing, parallel investigators, and even a second LLM model (OpenAI Codex) provide no benefit over greedy hill climbing while requiring 2-3x more evaluations. In our setting, the LLM's learned prior appears strong enough that acceptance-rule sophistication has limited impact--round 1 alone delivers the majority of improvement, and variants converge to similar configurations across strategies. The practical implication is surprising simplicity: greedy hill climbing with early stopping is a strong default. Beyond accuracy, the framework produces human-interpretable artifacts--the discovered cancer classification rules independently recapitulate established cytopathology principles.

zh.title: 贪心算法是强大的默认方法：代理作为迭代优化器
zh.summary: 通过用LLM引导的推理替代随机提议，该论文展示了贪心爬山法与模拟退火等复杂方法相当或超越。跨任务的结果表明LLM先验足够强大，接受规则的复杂性提供的收益有限。

fr.title: Le greedy est un fort défaut : les agents en tant qu'optimiseurs itératifs
fr.summary: En remplaçant les propositions aléatoires par un raisonnement guidé par LLM dans les algorithmes d'optimisation classiques, l'article montre que l'escalade gloutonne égale ou dépasse des méthodes complexes comme le recuit simulé. Les résultats sur les tâches suggèrent que les priors LLM sont suffisamment forts pour que la sophistication dans les règles d'acceptation offre un bénéfice limité.

de.title: Greedy ist ein starker Standard: Agenten als iterative Optimierer
de.summary: Durch Ersetzen von zufälligen Vorschlägen durch LLM-gesteuertes Denken in klassischen Optimierungsalgorithmen zeigt das Paper, dass greedy hill climbing komplexere Methoden wie simulated annealing entspricht oder übertrifft. Ergebnisse über Tasks deuten darauf hin, dass LLM-Priors stark genug sind, dass Raffinesse in Akzeptanzregeln begrenzten Nutzen bietet.

es.title: Greedy es un valor por defecto fuerte: agentes como optimizadores iterativos
es.summary: Al reemplazar propuestas aleatorias con razonamiento guiado por LLM en algoritmos de optimización clásicos, el artículo muestra que el hill climbing codicioso iguala o supera métodos complejos como el recocido simulado. Los resultados en tareas sugieren que los priors de LLM son lo suficientemente fuertes como para que la sofisticación en las reglas de aceptación proporcione beneficios limitados.

title: AstraAI: LLMs, Retrieval, and AST-Guided Assistance for HPC Codebases
summary: AstraAI is a CLI framework for HPC software development that combines large language models with retrieval-augmented generation and abstract syntax tree analysis. The system generates context-aware code modifications that maintain structural consistency with existing codebases.
content:
arXiv:2603.27423v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present AstraAI, a command-line interface (CLI) coding framework for high-performance computing (HPC) software development. AstraAI operates directly within a Linux terminal and integrates large language models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Abstract Syntax Tree (AST)-based structural analysis to enable context-aware code generation for complex scientific codebases. The central idea is to construct a high-fidelity prompt that is passed to the LLM for inference. This prompt augments the user request with relevant code snippets retrieved from the underlying framework codebase via RAG and structural context extracted from AST analysis, providing the model with precise information about relevant functions, data structures, and overall code organization. The framework is designed to perform scoped modifications to source code while preserving structural consistency with the surrounding code. AstraAI supports both locally hosted models from Hugging Face and API-based frontier models accessible via the American Science Cloud, enabling flexible deployment across HPC environments. The system generates code that aligns with existing project structures and programming patterns. We demonstrate AstraAI on representative HPC code generation tasks within AMReX, a DOE-supported HPC software infrastructure for exascale applications.

zh.title: AstraAI：面向HPC代码库的LLM、检索和AST引导协助
zh.summary: AstraAI是一个用于HPC软件开发的CLI框架，结合了大语言模型、检索增强生成和抽象语法树分析。该系统生成上下文感知的代码修改，保持与现有代码库的结构一致性。

fr.title: AstraAI : LLMs, Récupération et Assistance Guidée par AST pour les Bases de Code HPC
fr.summary: AstraAI est un framework CLI pour le développement de logiciels HPC qui combine des modèles de langage volumineux avec la génération augmentée par récupération et l'analyse syntaxique abstraite. Le système génère des modifications de code conscientes du contexte qui maintiennent la cohérence structurelle avec les bases de code existantes.

de.title: AstraAI: LLMs, Retrieval und AST-gesteuerte Unterstützung für HPC-Codebases
de.summary: AstraAI ist ein CLI-Framework für die HPC-Softwareentwicklung, das große Sprachmodelle mit retrieval-augmentierter Generierung und Analyse abstrakter Syntaxbäume kombiniert. Das System generiert kontextbewusste Codemodifikationen, die strukturelle Konsistenz mit bestehenden Codebases bewahren.

es.title: AstraAI: LLMs, Recuperación y Asistencia Guiada por AST para Bases de Código HPC
es.summary: AstraAI es un framework CLI para desarrollo de software HPC que combina modelos de lenguaje grandes con generación aumentada por recuperación y análisis de árbol de sintaxis abstracto. El sistema genera modificaciones de código conscientes del contexto que mantienen consistencia estructural con bases de código existentes.

title: The Novelty Bottleneck: A Framework for Understanding Human Effort Scaling in AI-Assisted Work
summary: The paper proposes a theoretical model where human effort in AI-assisted tasks scales linearly with the fraction of decisions requiring human judgment, analogous to Amdahl's Law. The framework explains empirical observations in coding benchmarks and productivity data.
content:
arXiv:2603.27438v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We propose a stylized model of human-AI collaboration that isolates a mechanism we call the novelty bottleneck: the fraction of a task requiring human judgment creates an irreducible serial component analogous to Amdahl's Law in parallel computing. The model assumes that tasks decompose into atomic decisions, a fraction $\nu$ of which are "novel" (not covered by the agent's prior), and that specification, verification, and error correction each scale with task size. From these assumptions, we derive several non-obvious consequences: (1) there is no smooth sublinear regime for human effort it transitions sharply from $O(E)$ to $O(1)$ with no intermediate scaling class; (2) better agents improve the coefficient on human effort but not the exponent; (3) for organizations of n humans with AI agents, optimal team size decreases with agent capability; (4) wall-clock time achieves $O(\sqrt{E})$ through team parallelism but total human effort remains $O(E)$; and (5) the resulting AI safety profile is asymmetric -- AI is bottlenecked on frontier research but unbottlenecked on exploiting existing knowledge. We show these predictions are consistent with empirical observations from AI coding benchmarks, scientific productivity data, and practitioner reports. Our contribution is not a proof that human effort must scale linearly, but a framework that identifies the novelty fraction as the key parameter governing AI-assisted productivity, and derives consequences that clarify -- rather than refute -- prevalent narratives about intelligence explosions and the "country of geniuses in a data center."

zh.title: 新颖性瓶颈：理解AI辅助工作中人力付出扩展的框架
zh.summary: 本论文提出了一个理论模型，其中AI辅助任务中的人力付出与需要人类判断的决策比例呈线性关系，类似于阿姆达尔定律。该框架解释了编码基准和生产力数据中的实证观察。

fr.title: Le Goulot d'Étranglement de la Nouveauté : Un Cadre pour Comprendre la Mise à l'Échelle de l'Effort Humain dans le Travail Assisté par l'IA
fr.summary: L'article propose un modèle théorique où l'effort humain dans les tâches assistées par l'IA évolue linéairement avec la fraction de décisions nécessitant un jugement humain, analogues à la Loi d'Amdahl. Le cadre explique les observations empiriques dans les benchmarks de codage et les données de productivité.

de.title: Der Neuheitsbottleneck: Ein Rahmenwerk zum Verständnis der Skalierung menschlicher Anstrengungen in KI-gestützter Arbeit
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt ein theoretisches Modell vor, bei dem die menschliche Anstrengung in KI-unterstützten Aufgaben linear mit dem Anteil der Entscheidungen skaliert, die menschliches Urteilsvermögen erfordern, analog zu Amdahls Gesetz. Der Rahmen erklärt empirische Beobachtungen in Codierungsbenchmarks und Produktivitätsdaten.

es.title: El Cuello de Botella de la Novedad: Un Marco para Entender la Escalabilidad del Esfuerzo Humano en el Trabajo Asistido por IA
es.summary: El artículo propone un modelo teórico donde el esfuerzo humano en tareas asistidas por IA se escala linealmente con la fracción de decisiones que requieren juicio humano, análogo a la Ley de Amdahl. El marco explica observaciones empíricas en benchmarks de codificación y datos de productividad.

title: PeopleSearchBench: A Multi-Dimensional Benchmark for Evaluating AI-Powered People Search Platforms
summary: PeopleSearchBench evaluates AI-powered people search platforms using criteria-grounded verification that checks whether returned profiles satisfy factual requirements. The benchmark tests four use cases and finds significant performance differences across platforms on relevance and coverage.
content:
arXiv:2603.27476v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI-powered people search platforms are increasingly used in recruiting, sales prospecting, and professional networking, yet no widely accepted benchmark exists for evaluating their performance. We introduce PeopleSearchBench, an open-source benchmark that compares four people search platforms on 119 real-world queries across four use cases: corporate recruiting, B2B sales prospecting, expert search with deterministic answers, and influencer/KOL discovery. A key contribution is Criteria-Grounded Verification, a factual relevance pipeline that extracts explicit, verifiable criteria from each query and uses live web search to determine whether returned people satisfy them. This produces binary relevance judgments grounded in factual verification rather than subjective holistic LLM-as-judge scores. We evaluate systems on three dimensions: Relevance Precision (padded nDCG@10), Effective Coverage (task completion and qualified result yield), and Information Utility (profile completeness and usefulness), averaged equally into an overall score. Lessie, a specialized AI people search agent, performs best overall, scoring 65.2, 18.5% higher than the second-ranked system, and is the only system to achieve 100% task completion across all 119 queries. We also report confidence intervals, human validation of the verification pipeline (Cohen's kappa = 0.84), ablations, and full documentation of queries, prompts, and normalization procedures. Code, query definitions, and aggregated results are available on GitHub.

zh.title: PeopleSearchBench：评估AI驱动人员搜索平台的多维基准
zh.summary: PeopleSearchBench使用基于标准的验证来评估AI驱动的人员搜索平台，检查返回的配置文件是否满足事实要求。该基准测试了四个用例，并发现平台在相关性和覆盖范围上的性能差异显著。

fr.title: PeopleSearchBench : Un Benchmark Multi-Dimensionnel pour Évaluer les Plateformes de Recherche de Personnes Alimentées par l'IA
fr.summary: PeopleSearchBench évalue les plateformes de recherche de personnes alimentées par l'IA en utilisant une vérification basée sur des critères qui vérifie si les profils renvoyés satisfont aux exigences factuelles. Le benchmark teste quatre cas d'utilisation et trouve des différences de performance importantes entre les plateformes en termes de pertinence et de couverture.

de.title: PeopleSearchBench: Ein mehrdimensionales Benchmark zur Bewertung von KI-gestützten Personensuchplattformen
de.summary: PeopleSearchBench bewertet KI-gestützte Personensuchplattformen mithilfe kriteriengestützter Verifizierung, die überprüft, ob zurückgegebene Profile faktische Anforderungen erfüllen. Die Benchmark testet vier Anwendungsfälle und findet erhebliche Leistungsunterschiede zwischen Plattformen bei Relevanz und Abdeckung.

es.title: PeopleSearchBench: Un Benchmark Multidimensional para Evaluar Plataformas de Búsqueda de Personas Impulsadas por IA
es.summary: PeopleSearchBench evalúa plataformas de búsqueda de personas impulsadas por IA utilizando verificación basada en criterios que verifica si los perfiles devueltos satisfacen requisitos factuales. La prueba de referencia prueba cuatro casos de uso y encuentra diferencias de rendimiento significativas entre plataformas en relevancia y cobertura.
